choice: Yes — Asset umbrella with synced and manual kinds plus bucket incl. non-drawable
rationale: Decision b6012bc6 explicitly names rental income as a stream that attaches to an Asset, and rental property is archetypically a manually-valued, non-drawable asset. This means the domain model already requires an Asset concept broader than just E*TRADE brokerage accounts. Decision 8a2f4e15 constrains that 'all tracked [investment] accounts are at E*TRADE,' but it does not assert that investment accounts are the only kind of asset — it defines two drawdown buckets for the simulation, not an exhaustive asset taxonomy. Together, these priors strongly imply: (1) a general Asset type exists, (2) some assets (E*TRADE accounts) are synced and map to the tax-deferred/post-tax buckets, (3) others (e.g., real estate) are manually valued and may carry income streams but are not drawable simulation buckets. A non-drawable manual kind is the only coherent way to reconcile both decisions.
